subject: Physics
title: What Feynman Really Meant
description: A 60-second lesson on What Feynman Really Meant.

hook.text: Richard Feynman famously said: 'If you think you understand quantum mechanics, you don't.'

buildup.text: Feynman wasn't admitting defeat. He was saying quantum mechanics defies everyday intuition by design.

discovery.text: He developed path integrals—a framework where particles take every possible path simultaneously.

twist.text: Feynman's diagrams made quantum calculations visual and tractable. They're still used daily in labs.

climax.text: His point stands: quantum mechanics works perfectly. Understanding it intuitively may be impossible.

punchline.text: Nobody understands it. Everybody uses it. Welcome to quantum.

quiz.question: What was Feynman's key contribution to quantum mechanics?
options: Path integrals and Feynman diagrams for quantum calculations
